fields.two_mac_result: 5/5 basic_v1 prompts: distributed greedy output token-identical to single-node camelid (master layers 0..24 on the primary M4 16GB, worker layers 24..48 + output head on the second M4 16GB). 3/5 prompts additionally match the pinned llama.cpp comparator full-budget; haiku-sea and rust-fn carry recorded reference-comparator frontiers (no sound bit-exact comparator mode exists for this row — the reference's -ub 1 GEMV path miscomputes the V-less architecture and its batched-prefill GEMM wobbles knife-edges; bisected and quoted in the oracle artifact)
fields.two_mac_stats: decode 6.17-6.75 tok/s across the pair; activation payload 15384 B/step; wire 79.8-119.7 ms/step avg; TTFT 157-161s (one-time cold mmap faults on the USB-SSD master shard); master RSS 6.4-6.8GB, worker RSS 6.7-8.0GB — both within budget for a 12.67GB row that is memory-infeasible single-node on the primary host (raw abort log included)
fields.network: master->worker over LAN (gigabit/WiFi mix; the Thunderbolt-class link-local route measured 0.78ms vs 17ms LAN but flapped under sustained load; connect retries recorded)
fields.claim: distributed layer sharding for memory headroom — NOT shared memory, NOT a throughput claim
fields.host: primary: Mac mini M4 16GB (model on USB SSD); worker: Mac mini M4 16GB (model on internal NVMe)

claim_boundary: Exact-row text-token generation within the checked basic_v1 prompt pack and API smoke envelope only. No bounded-context promotion, no performance claim beyond the raw logs, no multimodal input (fails closed with a typed error), no neighboring-row or Gemma-family support, no shared-memory claim (distributed mode is layer sharding).
